action.hypothesis: The optimizer and aux-loss stages did not improve on the `topk` signum anchor, so the next mainline question is whether a slightly smaller support can preserve most of the reconstruction while improving LUTurbo deployment cost. Reducing `K` from `128` to `96` is the least aggressive first step on the K axis and should reveal whether the current topk recipe has obvious over-allocation in support size.
action.summary: Keep the `topk` mainline recipe fixed at `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, and the original auxiliary setting, and change only `K` from `128` to `96`. This starts the K stage with the nearest lower allowed value, so the run cleanly answers whether LUTurbo can gain a cost-friendly support reduction before quality drops sharply.
action.self_review: This follows the prescribed order: LR and optimizer were explored, the aux-loss probe did not improve the frontier, and now the search moves to K. The main risk is that `K=96` may be too close to `K=128` to unlock a meaningful deployment gain, but it is still the most interpretable first K move and gives a clean slope estimate for the cost-quality tradeoff.
notes_to_memory: `topk` remains the active mainline family, with the current best EF=12 anchor at `K=128`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, `FVU=0.4522051215171814`.
The `adam` optimizer probe regressed and the aux-loss removal probe failed to improve, so the next justified mainline step is the K stage.
Round 7 begins K exploration with the smallest possible move from the anchor, `K=96`, to estimate the local cost-quality slope before taking larger support cuts.
next_hypotheses: If `K=96` stays close to the `K=128` anchor with a healthy curve, continue down to `K=64` on the same recipe to look for a real LUTurbo cost win.
If `K=96` regresses sharply, treat the current topk recipe as support-hungry and consider whether a new family is needed rather than forcing smaller K.
If `K=96` is mixed, compare curve shape and late slope to decide whether the degradation is true capacity loss or a sign that the lower-K regime needs its own LR retune.

result.description: Keep the `topk` mainline recipe fixed at `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, and the original auxiliary setting, and change only `K` from `128` to `96`. This starts the K stage with the nearest lower allowed value, so the run cleanly answers whether LUTurbo can gain a cost-friendly support reduction before quality drops sharply.
result.self_review: This follows the prescribed order: LR and optimizer were explored, the aux-loss probe did not improve the frontier, and now the search moves to K. The main risk is that `K=96` may be too close to `K=128` to unlock a meaningful deployment gain, but it is still the most interpretable first K move and gives a clean slope estimate for the cost-quality tradeoff.
